title: microcybe pauciflora
intro: Microcybe pauciflora, also known as Western Yew or Western Red Cedar, is a coniferous tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which contain compounds like terpinen-4-ol and alpha-pinene, that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Microcybe pauciflora include its ability to reduce stress, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. Herbal preparations of the plant include essential oil extracts, which can be used in aromatherapy, as well as tea infusions and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation and relieve respiratory issues.
